test.name: Evidence classification request should not trigger conflict-resolution
test.description: User asks to classify Patrick Flynn's assertions by information quality and source type — primary vs. secondary, original vs. derivative. Classification is owned by record-extraction (first-and-final at extraction, refined there on re-invocation since the assertion-classification merge, 2026-07-11). Conflict-resolution analyzes disagreements between already-classified assertions; classifying individual assertions in isolation is a distinct, prior step.

input.user_message: Go through Patrick Flynn's assertions and classify each piece of evidence — which are primary sources and which are secondary? Which are original documents versus derivatives?

negative.explanation: The user wants individual quality ratings for each assertion — information_quality and source_classification fields. Those classifications are owned by record-extraction, which writes them at extraction and refines them on request (the former assertion-classification skill was merged into it). Conflict-resolution enters when two or more classified assertions disagree on a value; the classification work comes first.

judge_context: Should route to record-extraction (which owns evidence classification since the assertion-classification merge) rather than starting a conflict analysis
The tell: the user is asking for individual quality labels (primary/secondary, original/derivative), not asking which source wins a disagreement
